subparts.text: What is the percentage breakdown of the factors that determine our general happiness? i. 50% Genetic Setpoint, 50% Life Circumstances ii. 40% Genetic Setpoint, 60% Life Circumstances iii. 50% Genetic Setpoint, 10% Life Circumstances, and 40% Our Intentional Actions and Thoughts iv. 50% Genetic Setpoint, 40% Life Circumstances, and 10% Our Intentional Actions and Thoughts

What does PERMA stand for? i. Positive Emotions, Empathy, Relationships, Meaning, Actualisation ii. Positive Emotions, Engagement, Relationships, Meaning, Authenticity iii. Positive Emotions, Energy, Relationships, Mental Health, Achievement iv. Positive Emotions, Engagement, Relationships, Meaning, Achievement
________ proposed the hierarchy of needs. i. William James ii. David McClelland iii. Abraham Maslow iv. Albert Bandura

If you had some spare money, spending money shopping might induce a sense of ________ while helping the needy would induce a sense of __________? i. happiness; contentment ii. pleasure; gratification iii. materialism; spirituality iv. temporary optimism; more long-lasting optimism
The pleasures are concerned with i. eudaimonia ii. the bodily senses and the emotions and positive perceptions iii. enacting personal strengths and virtues iv. both ‘a’ and ‘b’ above

Which of the following would be an example of a shortcut to the pleasures that might result in a neglect of the gratifications i. eating food that pleases the palate ii. watching TV/movies that have high entertainment value iii. drinking alcohol or taking recreational drugs iv. all of the above
Who is the father of Positive Psychology? i. Martin Seligman ii. Carl Rojers iii. Stevenson iv. None of the above

____________ refers to how well we are equipped to cope with the problems of life. i. Livability of environment ii. Life-ability of the person iii. Utility of life iv. Satisfaction i) Compared to pessimists, optimists tend to i. Be less able to cope with stress ii. Experience more distress iii. Doubt about solving the problem iv. Be confident about solving the problem j) When writing out your goals, what are the top 4 things you should keep in mind? i. Money , time vacation and resources ii. Benefits, challenges, strategies and rewards iii. Mind, Body ,Spirit and Other people iv. None of the above k) What is the 'flow state'? i. Being relaxed going and 'going with the flow' ii. Holding a optimistic mindset iii. Overcoming from challenges iv. Being totally captivated and engrossed in something l) The ecstatic kind of satisfaction characterized as a passing experience which is intense, pervasive and 'oceanic', is usually referred to as ____________ i. Pleasure ii. Part satisfaction iii. Top experience iv. Life satisfaction m) Creating a detailed strategy plan for your goals will help you to______ i. Understand and implement each task ii. Give you a start and end date iii. Identify your team and resources iv. All of the above n) Considering the research studies, which of the options below closely aligns with the comparison of PREDICTED emotions to ACTUAL emotions after receiving bad news like getting a bad grade or getting turned down for a job offer. i. 😐 Neutral , 😐 Neutral ii. 😐 Neutral , 😭 Super Sad iii. 😭 Super Sad , 😭 Super Sad iv. 😭 Super Sad , 😐 Neutral o) Which of the following is an example of intrinsic motivation? i. Completing the project to impress the teacher ii. Playing a musical instrument for personal enjoyment iii. Studying to avoid being scolded iv. Participating in a quiz for money p) When you do a google search for "[Social Media Platform] makes me feel” you get AutoFill searches as given below. Which of the following features of our mind best fits these patterns? i. Our minds’ strongest intuitions are often totally wrong ii. Our minds don’t think in terms of absolutes; our minds judge to relative reference points iii. Our minds are built to get used to stuff iv. We don’t realize that our minds are built to get used to stuff q) The word affect refers to? i. Emotions ii. Drives iii. Actively pursuing your goals iv. The opposite of cause r) On average, people would rather make less if their peers earned less over being paid more if their peers made more. Which of the following features of our mindset supports the given statement? i. Our minds’ strongest intuitions are often totally wrong ii. Our minds don’t think in terms of absolutes; our minds judge to relative reference points iii. Our minds are built to get used to stuff iv. We don’t realize that our minds are built to get used to stuff s) Being able to successfully recover from negative life events is known as? i. Resilience ii. Positive effect iii. Coping iv. None of the above t) What does the term well-being include? i. Well-being includes good physical health, a happy state of mind ii. Well-being comprises living in a big house and an affluent area iii. Well-being includes being liked by all your peers iv. None of the above

questions.question_text: How is happiness defined in positive psychology? Discuss its core characteristics and critically evaluate the major theoretical perspectives that explain the nature and pursuit of happiness.

Reflect on your personal experience with maintaining a gratitude journal. In what ways can the practice of gratitude journaling serve as a tool for emotional regulation and resilience during difficult times?
Analyze a scenario where a person pursues short-term pleasure at the expense of long-term happiness. What steps could they take to shift focus from pleasure to sustainable happiness?
Evaluate the statement: “Social media is the junk food of social life.” Do you agree or disagree? Provide arguments to support your viewpoint based on research and personal observation.

What is meant by the term “miswanting”? How does it affect human decision- making and life satisfaction? Illustrate your response with real-life examples and propose strategies to minimize the impact of miswanting.

Using Veenhoven’s Life Quality Matrix, examine the key determinants influencing life satisfaction among students at IGDTUW. What insights can be drawn from this framework in the context of student well-being?
How does adopting a fixed versus a growth mindset affect academic performance and emotional resilience among students? Discuss with examples.
What do you understand by “subjective well-being”? Describe the factors impacting the well-being of a person.
